content.cn_text: [旁白]: 第三部分。你将听到一位学生和她的教授讨论一项作业的对话。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Section 3. You will hear a conversation between a student and her professor discussing an assignment.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 首先，你有一些时间来查看问题21至25。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 现在请仔细听并回答问题21至25。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 汤普森博士，打扰一下，我能和您说几分钟话吗？
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Excuse me, Dr. Thompson, may I speak to you for a minute?

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 当然，请进。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: Of course, please come in.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 我是亚历山德拉·琼斯，我在这所大学学习商科。
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: I'm Alexandra Jones, I'm studying business here at the university.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 为了我的作业，我必须对那些拥有自己组织的人进行一项调查。
content.en_text: And for my assignment I have to carry out a survey of people who own their own organizations.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 我听说您是自己公司的创始人，我想知道是否可以向您请教一些问题。
content.en_text: I heard that you are the founder of your own company, and I was wondering if I could ask you some questions about it.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 是的，当然，请尽管问。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: Yes of course, please, fire away.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 好的，太好了，我们开始吧。您在创办公司时遇到过什么问题吗？
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Okay great, we'll get started. Did you encounter any problems whilst you were starting your company?

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 实际上，并没有我预期的那么困难。我一开始就有非常明确的目标，所以我清楚地知道我想要我的企业形象是什么。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: Actually, it was not as hard as I anticipated. I started out with very clear objectives, so I knew exactly what I wanted my corporate identity to be.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 由于伦敦的房地产市场竞争激烈，很难找到合适的场地，但我最终找到了完美的地点。
content.en_text: It was very difficult to find the correct premises as the property market in London is so competitive, but eventually I found the perfect site.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 最困难的部分是构建业务结构，因为我没有正式的模板，所以我必须自己创建。
content.en_text: The hardest part was structuring the business, as I had no formal template, so I had to create it myself.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 是的，这非常令人印象深刻。处理员工问题困难吗？
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Yes, that is very impressive. Was it difficult dealing with staff?

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 要满足一群兴趣各异的人总是很棘手的。不过，我没有遇到太多问题。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: It's always tricky trying to satisfy a group of people with varying interests. However, I didn't encounter many problems.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 我发现让员工感到被欣赏非常重要，所以我举办了一个员工日，让大家可以互相介绍，作为个体相互了解。
content.en_text: I found it very important to make the staff feel appreciated, so I hosted a staff day where we could all introduce ourselves and get to know each other as individuals.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 我也能够和他们谈论他们将要做的工作类型，并给了他们机会与我分享他们在工作分配上遇到的问题。
content.en_text: I was also able to talk to them about the type of work that they would be doing and gave them the opportunity to share with me the issues they had with the allocation of the work.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 好的，您是雇佣了一个管理团队还是自己担任经理的角色？
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Okay, did you hire a management team or did you take on the role of the manager?

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 哦不，我手头已经有足够多的任务要处理了。我雇佣了另一个人来担任经理的角色。然而，他在做决定前从不征求我的意见，
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: Oh no, I already had enough tasks to take care of. I employed someone else to take on the role of the manager. However, he never consulted me before making decisions,

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 所以我被迫解雇了他并雇佣了另一个人。
content.en_text: so I was forced to let him go and hire someone else.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 其他员工对新经理有什么看法？
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: And what did the other staff think of the new manager?

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 他们对他非常尊重。我雇佣他之前征求过他们的意见，这肯定很有帮助。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: They had a great deal of respect for him. It definitely helped that I consulted with them before hiring him.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 他们很惊讶，他在公司里扮演的角色如此成功，尽管会议时间更长了。
content.en_text: They were surprised that he has been so successful in his role at the company, despite the longer meeting hours.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 公司开业以来，有什么重大成就吗？
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Since the company opened, has it had any great achievements?

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 公司有很多方面都让我满意。然而，有一项成就让我特别自豪。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: There are many aspects of the company that I am pleased with. However, there is one achievement of which I am particularly proud.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 我想画一幅壁画来美化我们的入口区域，所以我决定邀请当地小学为我们创作一幅，这将是个好主意。
content.en_text: I wanted a mural painted to brighten up our entrance area, so I decided it would be a great idea to invite the local primary school to create one for us.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 孩子们玩得很开心，我知道他们很高兴能参与其中。
content.en_text: The youngsters had a great time, and I know they were thrilled to be included.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 哦，那太棒了。
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Oh, that's lovely.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 在你听对话的其余部分之前，你有一些时间来查看问题26至30。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 26 to 30.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 现在请听并回答问题26至30。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 26 to 30.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 公司开业以来，您遇到过什么重大问题吗？
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Have you encountered any major problems since you opened the company?

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 是的，我们总是遇到雇佣问题，因为很难找到符合我们提供职位资格的人。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: Yes, we are always encountering issues with employment, because it is very hard to find people who are qualified for the roles that we are offering.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 但不幸的是，对此没有解决办法。
content.en_text: But unfortunately, there is no solution to this.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 我们的一位行政人员最近也休产假离开了，这让我们陷入了困境。然而，我们已经设法为她找到了一个临时替代人选。
content.en_text: One of our administrators also recently left to go on maternity leave, which put us in a tricky situation. However, we have managed to find a temporary replacement for her.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 现在也快到了我们必须进行财务培训的时期，所以我需要找一个有足够空间容纳所有人的场地。
content.en_text: It is also now approaching the period when we must carry out our financial training, so I need to find a venue with enough space to hold everyone.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 组织那次活动会很有趣。
content.en_text: It will be interesting trying to organise that event.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 哦，天哪，我能想象。我自己都很难组织好。
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Oh, gosh, I can imagine. I find it hard enough to organise myself.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 组织是一件很难掌握的事情，但它对成功也至关重要。在我学习如何保持有条理的时候，我意识到激励的作用至关重要。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: Organisation is a difficult thing to master, but it is also essential for success. Whilst I was learning how to stay organised, I realised that the role of motivation is essential.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 好的，那太棒了。
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Okay, that's great.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 我还建议你去图书馆看看，那里有一个关于文化的部分，其中会有一些非常有价值的资料，教你如何发展你的组织能力。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: I also suggest that you visit the library, as there is a section on culture that will have some very valuable material on how to develop your organisation skills.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 好的，我们结束后我就去图书馆。
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: Okay, I'll head to the library when we're finished.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 我想你会觉得它非常有用。他们还有一系列关于个人组织的纪录片，我建议你查阅关于变革管理的文献。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: I think you will find it very useful. They also have a collection of documentaries on the subject of personal organisation, and I suggest that you look up the literature on management of change.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 我个人觉得这些资料非常有帮助。文章也是一种宝贵的资源，我们的大学图书馆里有整整一套。
content.en_text: I personally found these sources incredibly useful. Articles are also a valuable resource, and we have a whole collection of them in our university library.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 我建议你查看第二部分，在那里你会找到关于志愿服务性质的文章。
content.en_text: I advise that you look at section 2, where you will find articles on the nature of volunteering.

content.cn_text: [亚历山德拉]: 太棒了。非常感谢您的帮助。
content.en_text: [Alexandra]: That's great. Thank you so much for your help.

content.cn_text: [汤普森博士]: 不客气。
content.en_text: [Dr. Thompson]: No problem.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 第三部分到此结束。你现在有半分钟时间检查你的答案。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: That is the end of section 3. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 现在请翻到第四部分。
content.en_text: Now turn to section 4.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Question 21-25.

items.title: What was the most difficult problem when the company was founded?
What is the staff unsatisfied with?
What was the problem of the manager?

options.title: A
options.content: He had a lot of tasks to do.

options.title: B
options.content: He employed too many people and let them go then.

options.title: C
options.content: He always made decisions by himself.

items.title: What does the staff think of the new manager of the company?

options.title: A
options.content: They are delighted to see that the meeting time is shorter.

options.title: C
options.content: They are amazed that he has done it well.

items.title: What achievement has the organization made already?

options.title: A
options.content: Children were involved in painting the entrance area.

options.title: C
options.content: lt built a local primary school.
